Summary / Title: User cannot login with valid credentials - page refreshes with no error message
Preconditions: Valid user account exists (username: testuser, password: Test@123)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter valid username: testuser
3. Enter valid password: Test@123
4. Click Sign-in button
Actual Result: Page refreshes and stays on login page. No error message displayed. User remains not logged in.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to dashboard/home page

Summary / Title: Login page vulnerable to SQL injection - authentication bypass possible
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. In Username field enter: ' OR '1'='1' --
3. In Password field enter: anything
4. Click Sign-in button
Actual Result: User is successfully logged in without providing valid credentials. SQL injection payload bypassed authentication.
Expected Result: Login fails with "Invalid username or password" message. System should sanitize input to prevent SQL injection.

Summary / Title: Password field accepts leading spaces - should be trimmed or rejected
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter valid username
3. Enter valid password with leading spaces (e.g., " Test@123")
4. Click Sign-in button
Actual Result: Login fails with "Invalid username or password" message because leading spaces are not trimmed
Expected Result: System should either trim leading spaces automatically or show validation message; login should succeed with correct password regardless of leading spaces

Summary / Title: No validation message when exceeding character limit in Username field
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Enter username exceeding maximum allowed length (e.g., 51 characters when max is 50)
3. Tab out of field or click Sign-in
Actual Result: System allows entering beyond limit OR no validation message appears; user can type unlimited characters
Expected Result: System prevents entering beyond maximum limit OR displays clear validation message indicating character limit exceeded

Summary / Title: Session does not timeout after idle period
Test Steps:
1. Login successfully
2. Leave application idle for configured timeout period (e.g., 10 minutes) 
3. Attempt to perform an action (e.g., click any button or navigate)
Actual Result: User session remains active; user can perform actions without re-authentication
Expected Result: Session expires after idle timeout; user is redirected to login page or prompted to re-authenticate

Summary / Title: Error message reveals whether username exists in system
Test Steps:
1. Attempt login with existing username + wrong password
2. Observe error message
3. Attempt login with non-existing username + any password
4. Compare both error messages
Actual Result:
Different error messages appear:
- Existing username: "Password incorrect"
- Non-existing username: "Username not found"
Expected Result: Both attempts display the same generic error message (e.g., "Invalid username or password"). System does not reveal whether username exists.

Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. In Username field enter: <script>alert('XSS')</script>
3. In Password field enter: any value
4. Click Sign-in button
Actual Result: JavaScript alert executes; XSS script is reflected on page
Expected Result: System sanitizes input; scripts are not executed; input is displayed as plain text

Summary / Title: Password stored in plain text in database
Preconditions: Database read access, ability to create new user
Test Steps:
1. Create new user via UI with password "Test@123"
2. Run SELECT query: SELECT username, password FROM users WHERE username = 'newuser'
3. View stored password value
Actual Result: Stored password appears as plain text: "Test@123"
Expected Result: Password is stored in encrypted/hashed format (e.g., bcrypt, SHA-256 with salt). Plain text storage is not acceptable.

Summary / Title: Blank fields do not show validation messages
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page
2. Leave both Username and Password fields blank
3. Click Sign-in button
Actual Result: Page refreshes or submits without any validation message; no indication that fields are required
Expected Result: Validation messages appear indicating both fields are required; login is prevented

Summary / Title: Focus does not move to invalid field after validation
Test Steps:
1. Leave Username blank
2. Enter valid password
3. Click Sign-in button
Actual Result: Focus stays on Sign-in button or moves to Password field instead of Username field
Expected Result: Focus automatically moves to the first invalid field (Username field) to allow user to correct error easily

Summary / Title: SSL certificate is expired or not properly implemented
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to login page via HTTPS
2. Click on padlock icon in browser address bar
3. Check SSL certificate details and expiration date
Actual Result: SSL certificate is expired OR browser shows "Not Secure" warning OR certificate is not valid
Expected Result: SSL certificate is valid, not expired, and properly implemented. Connection is secure with valid HTTPS.

Summary / Title: Browser back button after successful login takes user to login page
Test Steps:
1. Login successfully and navigate to dashboard
2. Click browser back button
Actual Result: User is taken back to login page; session appears to be logged out
Expected Result: User is not taken to logout mode; session remains active; user stays within authenticated area or is redirected appropriately
